ab: saka lavavalakenay yaVedai ku danenay saugakunga latalela mu Pingtung lacekecekelenay ikay dratane.
ch: 小時候我們家住在霧台，上學以後，就搬到了屏東，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Vedai. After I started school, we moved to Pingtung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: samwangaku ki daygake laki bulrwaku ku patharwakay ki kaudhane ki kacalrisyane, ala thingalaku makaelaeela ku ngiradekane ku kakudhane.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Lakamani, kayasane kay tatelratrlradhane ki kaladralrangedrangane, kwaku si kaka lamubalriunay kivavaga ki tarudrawdrange apia thingalenay ki bengkata.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: taramavalrulunga ku tarudrwdrang, ai ludrelane miakaw yaengecengeana.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: lakivagaku iniane taypyane ku thingalesu ku vaga, ngudradreadrekadhane macalrusi lukawriva, lamialri thingale ku swa Tayrezukuane, Kecyaneu, si matatubalre ki Dripung.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的魯凱族語說得很流利，也會說泰雅語、客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Rukai, as well as Atayal, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: sakivaganganaku alaka singkalavavalake kibulru Dridripungane ku vaga, ku taelrelreini swa Tayrezuku, ikay ki takauaunganeini lathingale ki swa Kecya, lakamani ku kalrigilini ku taykalrane ku vaga.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是泰雅族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Atayal, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: sama kivavaganay, tarudraudrang, lamatikay ki talwalupane ki sidruadrumane.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: unu, kainay lrimaka ngulre thingale, ai arasucinay sassing ala lrikilalranay pudridringai.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: saka lavavalakaku kainaku wathingale pangukay ki kakwakudhane ki kacalrisiane, kay takakacalrisyaneli yakay ku takalrumalaneli.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: saka kalranga ku thingaleli la arudradraranaku taka Ngudradrekadhaneli ku taukalrivilane.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為魯凱族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Rukai is unique and something to be proud of.
